title: Üsküdar University students ranked in top three in “Best in Design 2026”!
content:
Üsküdar University students ranked in top three in “Best in Design 2026”!

SDG tags related to the news

DOI

https://doi.org/10.32739/uha.id.88347

Students from Üsküdar University's Faculty of Communication, Visual Communication Design Department, Maryem Grich and Huda Narmouq, achieved significant success by ranking in the top three in the international “Best in Design 2026” design competition. The dyslexia awareness book titled “Through Their Eyes” won the admiration of the jury members with its original approach and high design quality. The students are also aimed to represent Üsküdar at exhibitions and events to be held in the Czech Republic within the scope of the Zlín Design Week as part of the competition program.

They ranked in the top three in the competition!

Visual Communication Design Department students Maryem Grich and Huda Narmouq ranked in the top three in the international “Best in Design 2026” design competition.

The dyslexia awareness book titled “Through Their Eyes,” which stood out in the competition with participants from different countries, attracted attention with its innovative approach and strong conceptual framework.

The project once again demonstrated the students' international design competence.

They are aimed to represent Üsküdar in the Czech Republic

The students are aimed to represent Üsküdar University at exhibitions and events to be held in the Czech Republic within the scope of the Zlín Design Week as part of the competition program.

The book aimed to increase dyslexia awareness

The book, aiming to increase dyslexia awareness, was developed with two fundamental design approaches. “Dyslexia-friendly design and empathy-focused design.” In this regard, it aimed to more effectively convey the reading difficulties experienced by individuals with dyslexia.

In the first section of the two-part book, dyslexia was addressed within an educational framework, while in the second section, the visual stress experienced by individuals with dyslexia was conveyed to the reader through typographic simulations. This approach aimed to not only inform the reader but also to allow them to experience this feeling.

This achievement significantly contributes to Üsküdar University’s international visibility in academic and design fields.

Üsküdar News Agency (ÜHA)
